title: allium ericetorum subsp. pseudosuaveolens
intro: Allium ericetorum subsp. pseudosuaveolens is a subspecies of wild garlic native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It belongs to the Allium genus, known for its onion-like characteristics and medicinal properties. This plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Traditionally, Allium ericetorum subsp. pseudosuaveolens has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to aid in cold and flu relief, as well as support respiratory health.
